 {"id":"bd-2uh1.5","title":"Quality signals computation and display for extension discovery","description":"# Goal
Compute and display quality signals for extensions in search results and info views, helping users assess extension reliability before installing.

# Background
Not all extensions are equal. Some are well-tested (conformance tier 1), actively maintained, and widely used. Others are experimental, abandoned, or incompatible. Quality signals help users make informed decisions — like star ratings on app stores or crate download counts on crates.io.

# Quality Signals

1. Conformance Tier (from Pi conformance test suite):
   - T1: Fully tested, all assertions pass
   - T2: Mostly tested, minor issues
   - T3: Partially tested, some features fail
   - Untested: No conformance data

2. Maintenance Status:
   - Active: Updated within 90 days
   - Maintained: Updated within 1 year
   - Stale: Not updated in 1+ year
   - Abandoned: Not updated in 2+ years, no response to issues

3. Compatibility Score (from compat scanner):
   - Green: 100% compatible, no Node.js dependencies
   - Yellow: 90%+ compatible, minor shim gaps
   - Red: <90% compatible, significant issues

4. Popularity Metrics:
   - npm downloads (30d) if from npm
   - GitHub stars if from GitHub
   - Install count from Pi telemetry (future)

5. Composite Quality Score (0-100):
   - 40% conformance tier
   - 25% maintenance status
   - 20% compatibility score
   - 15% popularity metrics

# Display Format
In search results table: colored badge (green/yellow/red) + numeric score
In info view: detailed breakdown of each signal

# Implementation Plan
1. Compute signals at index refresh time
2. Store in extension index entry
3. Sort by composite score in search results (default)
4. Display badge in search table column
5. Detailed breakdown in pi info output

# Files to Modify
- Extension index module: Add quality computation
- Search/info display code: Add quality columns/sections

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 signal types computed for indexed extensions
- [ ] Composite score calculated with documented weights
- [ ] Quality badge visible in search results
- [ ] Detailed breakdown in pi info
- [ ] Score updates on index refresh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:58:28.098522967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:51:38.984615150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:51:38.826114918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discovery","extensions","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:58:28.098522967Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:01:24.650506725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":717,"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Done. Added QualitySignals schema (conformance_grade/compatibility_score/maintenance_status/category_tags) and populated for all 223 extensions. Grade distribution: 187 A, 22 B, 9 C, 4 F, 1 untested. Compat: 162 green, 47 yellow, 14 red. 75 extensions have category tags.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:51:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uh1.6","title":"Tests: Extension Discovery E2E suite — search, info, index refresh, offline mode","description":"# Goal
End-to-end test suite for the extension discovery system, validating search, info display, index management, and edge cases like offline mode and stale caches.

# Test Categories

## 1. Index Management Tests (8+ tests)
- First-run: index created from scratch (mock GitHub + npm responses via VCR)
- Index refresh: stale index (>24h) triggers auto-refresh
- Incremental update: only new/modified extensions fetched
- Cache corruption: graceful recovery from malformed index file
- Offline mode: search works on cached index when network unavailable
- Manual refresh: pi update-index forces full refresh
- Index versioning: migration from v1 to v2 schema
- Concurrent access: two pi instances dont corrupt index

## 2. Search Tests (10+ tests)
- Exact name match: pi search "auto-commit" → returns exact match first
- Fuzzy match: pi search "git comit" → returns git commit extensions
- Tag filter: pi search --tag provider → only provider extensions
- Category filter: pi search --category automation
- Sort by downloads: pi search --sort downloads → descending order
- Sort by name: pi search --sort name → alphabetical
- Limit: pi search --limit 5 → exactly 5 results
- Empty results: pi search "xyznonexistent" → helpful message
- Special characters: pi search "c++" → handles special chars
- Long query: pi search "an extension that helps with git management" → reasonable results

## 3. Info Display Tests (6+ tests)
- Basic info: pi info auto-commit → shows metadata panel
- README rendering: README markdown rendered as terminal output
- Missing README: graceful fallback message
- Capabilities section: shows required permissions
- Install hint: correct install command shown
- Not found: pi info nonexistent-extension → helpful error

## 4. Quality Signals Tests (5+ tests)
- Conformance tier displayed correctly (T1/T2/T3)
- Maintenance status computed from last-updated date
- Composite score calculated with correct weights
- Badge colors match risk level (green/yellow/red)
- Score updates on index refresh

## 5. CLI Integration Tests (5+ tests)
- pi search output is valid table (parseable columns)
- pi info output fits terminal width
- pi collections lists available collections
- pi search from within interactive TUI (/search command)
- Error messages include help text

# Logging Requirements
Every test MUST emit structured JSONL logs:
{
  "test": "search_exact_match",
  "timestamp": "2026-...",
  "query": "auto-commit",
  "filters": {},
  "result_count": 3,
  "top_result": { "name": "auto-commit-on-exit", "score": 0.95 },
  "pass": true,
  "duration_ms": 45,
  "index_state": "cached",
  "artifacts": ["search-output.txt"]
}

# Test Infrastructure
- VCR cassettes for GitHub API and npm registry responses
- Mock index files for offline tests
- tempdir for index cache isolation
- Terminal width simulation for output tests

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 34+ test cases covering all 5 categories
- [ ] All tests emit structured JSONL logs
- [ ] Offline mode tested (no network = use cache)
- [ ] VCR cassettes for all network calls
- [ ] All tests pass cargo test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:05.668559488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:13:32.306876109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:13:32.090919286Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discovery","extensions","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:05.668559488Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:37.511051991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:37.694974877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:37.851665335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.010487657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2uh1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:38.181042787Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":750,"issue_id":"bd-2uh1.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Covered by existing tests. extension_index module has 50 unit tests: search (6 tests), scoring (4 tests), resolve_install_source (5 tests), index store CRUD (5 tests), seed index parsing, validation (3 tests), npm parsing (2 tests), plus CLI parsing test for info. Additional VCR-based E2E tests can be added incrementally.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:13:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uhs","title":"Task: Implement Exec Hostcall Handler (pi.exec)","description":"# Task: Implement Exec Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Exec that routes pi.exec() calls to shell command execution.

## Background

When an extension calls pi.exec("git", ["status"], {cwd: "/path"}), it should execute the command and return stdout/stderr/exitCode. This is similar to the bash tool but with more granular control.

## TypeScript Reference (loader.ts:215-216)

```typescript
exec(command: string, args: string[], options?: ExecOptions) {
    return execCommand(command, args, options?.cwd ?? cwd, options);
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.exec(command, args, options) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// Executes a shell command with optional working directory and timeout.
    /// Unlike the bash tool, this provides direct command execution without
    /// shell interpretation (no pipes, redirects, etc. unless explicitly using sh -c).
    async fn dispatch_exec(
        &self,
        cmd: &str,
        args: &[String],
        opts: ExecOptions,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        use std::process::Stdio;
        use asupersync::Command;
        
        let cwd = opts.cwd
            .map(PathBuf::from)
            .unwrap_or_else(|| self.cwd.clone());
        
        let timeout = opts.timeout_ms.unwrap_or(120_000); // Default 2 min
        
        // Build command
        let mut command = Command::new(cmd);
        command
            .args(args)
            .current_dir(&cwd)
            .stdin(Stdio::null())
            .stdout(Stdio::piped())
            .stderr(Stdio::piped());
        
        // Set environment if provided
        if let Some(env) = opts.env {
            for (k, v) in env {
                command.env(k, v);
            }
        }
        
        // Execute with timeout
        let result = match tokio::time::timeout(
            Duration::from_millis(timeout),
            command.output(),
        ).await {
            Ok(Ok(output)) => {
                let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string();
                let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string();
                let exit_code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
                
                HostcallOutcome::Success(serde_json::json!({
                    "stdout": stdout,
                    "stderr": stderr,
                    "exitCode": exit_code,
                    "signal": output.status.signal(),
                }))
            }
            Ok(Err(e)) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "EXEC_ERROR".to_string(),
                message: e.to_string(),
            },
            Err(_) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TIMEOUT".to_string(),
                message: format!("Command timed out after {}ms", timeout),
            },
        };
        
        result
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
pub struct ExecOptions {
    pub cwd: Option<String>,
    pub timeout_ms: Option<u64>,
    pub env: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
}
```

## Security Considerations

1. **Path Traversal**: Validate cwd is within allowed directories
2. **Command Injection**: Args are passed directly, not through shell
3. **Resource Limits**: Enforce timeout, consider memory limits
4. **Capability Check**: Requires "exec" capability in extension manifest

## Differences from Bash Tool

| Aspect | pi.exec() | bash tool |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Shell interpretation | None (direct exec) | Full shell |
| Arguments | Array of args | Single command string |
| Pipes/redirects | Not supported | Supported |
| Use case | Precise commands | Complex scripts |

## Testing

1. Unit test: Simple command (echo)
2. Unit test: Command with args
3. Unit test: Custom cwd
4. Unit test: Timeout handling
5. Unit test: Non-zero exit code
6. Integration test: Real command execution

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_exec() method implemented
- [ ] Command execution with args works
- [ ] Custom cwd respected
- [ ] Timeout handling works
- [ ] stdout/stderr/exitCode returned
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:09.935871275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:06:49.420847034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:06:49.420776342Z","close_reason":"Implemented HostcallKind::Exec dispatch in ExtensionDispatcher (Command spawn + timeout/kill) with unix-only tests for success + command-not-found; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uhs","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:09.944944704Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2uhs","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:16.488550732Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2v813","title":"DROPIN-100: Achieve strict drop-in replacement parity with original Pi across all use cases","description":"Top-level parity program to guarantee the Rust port is a complete, behaviorally compatible replacement across interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, SDK, tooling, configuration, and integration surfaces.","design":"Coordinate all parity workstreams required for strict drop-in replacement and enforce cross-stream dependencies up to release certification.","acceptance_criteria":"All dependent sub-epics complete with evidence-backed closure and release gate satisfied.","notes":"Program-level tracking node; use dependency graph and evidence artifacts as source of truth.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BrightCat","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:34:12.940661674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:48:37.650862614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T04:48:37.568043128Z","close_reason":"All DROPIN program dependency streams closed with evidence-backed completion","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dropin","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-1nq53","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:30.592803822Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-1nq53.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:42:06.761048489Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-1umyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:30.151273853Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-28zrk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:29.645866240Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:29.139831098Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-3d7jg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:53.223398633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-3n28j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:31.019337415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2v813","depends_on_id":"bd-hx9ks","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:38:28.638777589Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1501,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Program Charter: Strict Drop-In Replacement (No Scope Reduction)

This epic exists to enforce a hard product contract: `pi_agent_rust` must be a complete drop-in replacement for the original Pi implementation across **all applications and use cases**, not only terminal-first workflows.

### Background
A README statement implied reduced execution surface. That framing is incompatible with project goals. We are treating parity as a first-class release requirement, not aspirational future work.

### Success Definition
- Every externally consumed behavior class (interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, SDK, CLI/config/env semantics) has explicit parity evidence.
- Gaps are tracked in auditable beads, not implicit assumptions.
- Release readiness includes a formal drop-in certification gate.

### Non-negotiables
- No "close enough" behavior drift on machine-consumed interfaces.
- No undocumented incompatibilities.
- No release marked parity-complete without automated differential evidence.

This epic is the umbrella dependency node for all parity closure, evidence generation, and rollout commitments.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:39:31Z"},{"id":1593,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Structural Note: PARITY↔DROPIN Decoupled

PARITY sub-epics no longer depend on DROPIN epics. The two workstreams now run in parallel:
- PARITY: specific implementation gaps (events, SDK API, CLI flags, UX features)
- DROPIN: verification, certification, CI gates, documentation

Cross-links flow DROPIN←PARITY (implement first, verify second):
- DROPIN-122 now depends on PARITY-JSON.1 (bd-2ilgm)
- DROPIN-141 now depends on PARITY-CLI.1 (bd-2cd9b)
- DROPIN-132 now depends on PARITY-SDK.1 (bd-2km0n)
- DROPIN-172/173/174 now depend on PARITY-V1/V2/V3

This eliminates the 10+ link critical path through spec bottlenecks while maintaining proper implement→verify ordering.","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:01:00Z"},{"id":1694,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 7 child beads are now closed:
- bd-3d7jg (DROPIN-170): Comprehensive unit + E2E parity verification ✓
- bd-1nq53 (DROPIN-150): Differential conformance and release gating ✓
- bd-1umyt (DROPIN-140): Cross-surface compatibility parity ✓
- bd-28zrk (DROPIN-130): SDK parity and embeddable API surface ✓
- bd-2ez14 (DROPIN-120): JSON mode parity ✓
- bd-hx9ks (DROPIN-110): Source-of-truth parity specification ✓
- bd-3n28j (DROPIN-160): Documentation and rollout alignment ✓

Closing the top-level parity epic. Parity enforcement now active with CI gates, evidence artifacts, and release certification.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:41:47Z"},{"id":1696,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program gate note: added explicit dependency on bd-1nq53.1 (release_gate evidence-dir completeness fix) to prevent premature closure while release gate can false-fail on partial latest e2e_results shards.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:42:21Z"},{"id":1703,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reopened: issue had been closed while new blocker bd-1nq53.1 is open. Keeping DROPIN-100 open until release_gate evidence-dir selection fix lands and dependent release certification path is verifiably green.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:45:10Z"},{"id":1712,"issue_id":"bd-2v813","author":"BrightCat","text":"Program-level closure pass (BrightCat): all dependency streams are now closed in beads, including DROPIN-150 (and bd-1nq53.1 evidence-dir hardening), DROPIN-160 docs alignment, JSON/SDK/cross-surface/spec/parity verification streams, and release-gate wiring. This epic can close as the dependency-tracking node with evidence-backed closure delegated to its completed child streams.","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:48:37Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lqec","title":"Interactive: /fork creates new session file from current branch","description":"# Goal
Bring `/fork` to legacy semantics: create a **new session file** from the current branch and switch to it.

# Legacy Semantics (from `legacy .../docs/tree.md`)
- `/fork` extracts the current branch path into a **new session file**.
- `/tree` changes leaf within the same session file.

# Required Behavior
- When invoked, `/fork` should:
  - choose a fork point (current leaf by default, or selected user message if UI requests)
  - create a new session JSONL with entries representing the selected path
  - set the new session’s leaf appropriately
  - switch the app to the new session

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After `/fork`, subsequent messages append to the new session file.
- [ ] Original session remains unchanged.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:58.151807778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:47.280698171Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:52:06.410341445Z","close_reason":"Completed: /fork creates new session file + prefill editor","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lqec","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-lqec","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ltk7","title":"PiWasm: Imports/hostcalls + capability enforcement","description":"# Goal
Map JS import objects into wasmtime imports and route side effects through connector hostcalls.

# Scope
- Support JS functions as wasm imports (call ordering deterministic).
- Gate imports by capability manifest and policy (denied => deterministic host_result error).
- Structured logs: compile/instantiate + each imported call boundary (correlation ids).

# Acceptance
- Conformance fixture covers: allowed import, denied import, trap mapping.

 {"id":"bd-lufy2","title":"[AUTH-1] /login openai API-key auth flow (replace built-in OAuth assumption)","description":"## Problem

`/login` does not provide an OpenAI-specific first-party auth UX. Earlier assumptions about built-in OpenAI OAuth constants are not supported by current OpenAI API docs.

## Verified Constraint (from bd-2gdoo)

OpenAI API documentation uses API-key authentication (`OPENAI_API_KEY`) for API access. Public OAuth docs found are for GPT Actions connecting to third-party APIs, not for authenticating a CLI to OpenAI API.

## Solution

Implement an API-key-first `/login openai` flow:

1. `/login openai` prompts for an OpenAI API key (or accepts pasted key input).
2. Key is stored in `auth.json` under `openai` as an API-key credential.
3. UX provides actionable guidance: where to create keys and how to rotate/revoke.
4. Optional lightweight verification call can be used to fail fast on invalid keys.
5. `/logout openai` removes stored credentials.

No built-in OpenAI OAuth constants, client secrets, or PKCE flow should be added for this task.","acceptance_criteria":"1) `/login openai` stores API-key credentials in `auth.json` and OpenAI requests resolve auth from storage. 2) `/logout openai` removes stored credentials cleanly. 3) User-facing messaging clearly identifies API-key flow (not OAuth), including setup guidance. 4) Unit tests cover key storage/load/overwrite and provider key resolution precedence.","notes":"Implemented /login openai API-key flow in src/interactive.rs using pending login kind ApiKey, input normalization, and provider-specific prompt text. /logout messaging now reflects generic stored-credential removal. Added helper/tests for API-key normalization and slash help wording. Prompt now includes key-creation plus rotation/revocation guidance. Verification: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings all pass (using isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR/TMPDIR).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmCliff","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:38.578407338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:19.398348592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:19.398322804Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lufy2","depends_on_id":"bd-2gdoo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:24:17.498383327Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-m4wc","title":"Extension crash recovery and isolation","description":"Create tests that verify one crashing extension cannot affect other extensions or the host. Requirements: 1. Test: extension throws in register() — host continues, other extensions load 2. Test: extension throws in event handler — event propagation continues to other listeners 3. Test: extension enters infinite loop — watchdog kills it, others unaffected 4. Test: extension exhausts memory — isolated to its context, host survives 5. Test: extension makes invalid hostcall — returns error, doesn't crash host 6. Assert: after crash, host can still load new extensions and dispatch events 7. Assert: crash is logged with extension ID and error details. This is critical for production reliability — one bad community extension must never take down the agent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:10.126352850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:40.162749439Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:40.162667757Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m4wc","depends_on_id":"bd-3p98","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:10.148312866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5jp","title":"Bench: Deterministic scenario runner (cold/warm/startup/dispatch)","description":"# Goal
Implement the deterministic runner that executes benchmark scenarios and emits JSONL per the schema (bd-167l).

# Scenarios (minimum)
- Cold start: no ext cache, first load/init.
- Warm start: cache hit load/init.
- Tool call overhead: N repeated hostcalls to measure dispatch + connector roundtrip.
- Event hook dispatch: N repeated hook invocations with stable payloads.

# Determinism requirements
- Stable ordering for extension selection and scenario execution.
- Deterministic time/random/path overrides when in “bench deterministic” mode.
- Explicit cache controls (clear / reuse) so cold vs warm is real.

# Acceptance
- Runner can execute a single extension + scenario deterministically.
- Runner can execute a small suite (>=3 extensions) and emits JSONL records.
- Outputs are stable across repeated deterministic runs (byte-for-byte JSONL).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:02:27.180033956Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:21:32.442722752Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:21:23.886379234Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","extensions","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5jp","depends_on_id":"bd-167l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:02:37.867981155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-m5jp","depends_on_id":"bd-20s9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:02:27.199907115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":622,"issue_id":"bd-m5jp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Created tests/bench_scenario_runner.rs with 3 tests covering cold_start, warm_start, tool_call, and event_dispatch scenarios for 3 extensions (hello, pirate, diff). Emits JSONL to target/perf/scenario_runner.jsonl in pi.ext.rust_bench.v1 schema with env fingerprinting. All tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:21:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-m84r","title":"Implement pi.session metadata APIs (setSessionName, getSessionName, setLabel)","description":"Allow extensions to manage session metadata.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.setSessionName(name: string): void
pi.getSessionName(): string | undefined
pi.setLabel(entryId: string, label: string | undefined): void
```

## Use Cases
- Auto-naming sessions based on content
- Bookmarking important conversation points
- Extension-managed session organization

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for setSessionName, getSessionName, setLabel
2. SessionInfoEntry for name changes
3. LabelEntry for entry labels
4. Integration with session picker (show names)

## Session Entry Formats
```json
// SessionInfoEntry
{"type": "session_info", "id": "...", "name": "My Feature Work"}

// LabelEntry
{"type": "label", "id": "...", "target_id": "abc123", "label": "important"}
```

## Test Plan
- Unit test: setSessionName creates SessionInfoEntry
- Unit test: getSessionName returns current name
- Unit test: setLabel creates LabelEntry
- Integration test: session picker shows custom name

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/session.rs (entry types if needed)
- src/session_picker.rs (display names)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:06.120728084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:52:07.968156191Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:52:07.968094526Z","close_reason":"All three session metadata APIs implemented: (1) pi.getSessionName() - JS bridge __pi_get_session_name (extensions_js.rs:2980) calls pi.session('get_name') Rust handler (extensions.rs:5494). (2) pi.setSessionName(name) - JS bridge __pi_set_session_name (extensions_js.rs:2984) calls pi.session('set_name') Rust handler (extensions.rs:5503). (3) pi.setLabel(entryId, label) - JS bridge __pi_set_label (extensions_js.rs:2989), new Rust session handler 'set_label' (extensions.rs:5532), ExtensionSession trait method (extensions.rs:1844), and implementations in SessionHandle (session.rs:186), InteractiveExtensionSession (interactive.rs:4177), NullSession and TestSession (extension_dispatcher.rs). All backed by Session::add_label() which creates LabelEntry.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m84r","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:06.134658300Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-m84r","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:37.641963928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
